narrative:
DOMINANT DRIVERS

1. USD H4 acceleration because CSM surged to 100 on H4 versus only 20 on D1, signaling a sharp intraday dollar bid overriding the broader risk-on trend
2. JPY broad weakness because USDJPY, EURJPY, GBPJPY, CADJPY, AUDJPY, NZDJPY all flash H4 sell signals in tight correlation cluster above 0.91, confirming systematic yen selling
3. Risk-on regime persistence because S&P 500 at 7230, gold at 4630, and copper firm collectively sustain appetite for risk currencies despite the concurrent dollar bid

PRICE CONFIRMS

GBPUSD is the clearest confirmation of the tension: D1 and H4 both show Buy signals yet GBP CSM is fading hard from 91 to 17, meaning the pair is being carried by JPY cross demand and dollar softness on the daily but being capped intraday by the H4 dollar surge. AUDUSD and NZDUSD mirror this with identical H4 Buy signals but collapsing CSM scores of 26 and 13 respectively, confirming they are lagging rather than leading the risk-on move. The USDCHF H4 Sell aligned with CHF CSM at 100 on D1 is the most structurally clean confirmation, as CHF strength and dollar weakness reinforce each other on that timeframe.

REGIME

Mixed, conflicted. VIX at 17 rising 0.6 percent argues against clean risk-on, S&P 500 rising and gold at record highs signals safe haven demand running alongside equities, and the H4 dollar surge directly contradicts the D1 risk-on classification.

CONTRADICTIONS

1. USD signal conflict: D1 regime is Risk-On with USD CSM at 20 yet H4 USD CSM has spiked to 100, making dollar direction incoherent across timeframes simultaneously
2. CHF versus risk-on: CHF ranks as the strongest currency at 100 on D1 while the regime is flagged Risk-On, safe haven demand at this magnitude is inconsistent with genuine risk appetite

TRADE IMPLICATIONS

Best: USDCHF down because D1 and H4 both Sell, CHF CSM dominant at 100, structurally cleanest alignment - Conviction 7/10
Secondary: GBPUSD up because D1 and H4 both Buy, GBP retains high D1 CSM, USD D1 weakness still intact - Conviction 6/10
Avoid: AUDUSD because AUD CSM fading sharply undermines H4 Buy signal reliability
Watch: H4 USD CSM reverting below 60 confirms dollar intraday bid exhaustion and validates both primary trades
key_observation: Mixed signals with USD rebounding on trade tensions despite broad risk-on sentiment evidenced by equity record highs and commodity strength. Agricultural commodities rallying sharply on supply concerns while energy softens on Iran negotiation hopes. One non-FX headline present.
watch: Trade tension escalation developments, US-Iran negotiation progress, and whether commodity strength can persist against dollar rebound momentum.
